title: Choosing a Movie
summary: Tom and Sara try to agree on a movie to watch together at home. They discuss their different preferences, argue about genres, and finally reach a compromise.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Hey Sara, do you want to watch a movie tonight? I don't have any plans.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Sure, sounds good! I was just thinking the same thing. What do you feel like watching?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: I really want to watch an action movie. There's a new one that just came out — it has great reviews.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Hmm, I'm not really in the mood for action tonight. I was thinking more of a comedy or maybe a romance.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: A romance? Come on, we always watch romances. Can't we try something different?

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: That's not true! Last time we watched that crime thriller you chose, and I didn't enjoy it at all.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Okay, fair enough. What about a comedy then? I'm okay with comedy.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: That works for me. Do you have anything specific in mind?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: There's that new one with the two friends who go on a road trip. I've heard it's really funny.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Oh, I've seen the trailer for that! It looked hilarious. How long is it?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: I think it's about one hour fifty minutes. So we'd finish around ten, which is fine.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Perfect. Can we make some popcorn? A movie isn't the same without popcorn.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Absolutely. I'll make it while you find the movie. Deal?

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Deal! I'll also grab some drinks from the fridge. See you in the living room in five minutes.

annotations.meaningVi: có tâm trạng muốn làm gì đó
annotations.exampleEn: I'm not in the mood for a loud party tonight — I'd rather stay home.
'I was late because my bus broke down.' 'Oh, fair enough.'
Do you have anything specific in mind for dinner?

annotations.meaningVi: chuyến đi bằng ô tô (thường dài và qua nhiều nơi)
annotations.exampleEn: They went on a road trip from Sydney to Melbourne and stopped at many small towns.
Have you seen the trailer for the new superhero movie?

annotations.meaningVi: cực kỳ buồn cười, hài hước
annotations.exampleEn: The comedian's routine was absolutely hilarious — everyone was in tears.
We always buy popcorn before the film starts.

annotations.meaningVi: Đồng ý không? Thỏa thuận không?
annotations.exampleEn: 'You wash up and I'll dry. Deal?' 'Deal!'

annotations.meaningVi: lấy nhanh (theo nghĩa thông thường, không phải bắt cóc)
annotations.exampleEn: Let me grab my jacket and then we can go.
He loves crime thrillers because they always keep him guessing.

annotations.meaningVi: sự thỏa hiệp, nhượng bộ cả hai phía
annotations.exampleEn: They reached a compromise and agreed to watch one action film and one comedy.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for recent events: 'I've heard', 'I've seen'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Hiện tại hoàn thành được dùng khi nói về kinh nghiệm hoặc thông tin không rõ thời gian cụ thể. 'I've heard it's really funny.' — Tôi đã nghe nói nó rất buồn cười (không nói rõ nghe khi nào). 'I've seen the trailer for that!' — Tôi đã xem trailer của phim đó rồi! Điều quan trọng là kinh nghiệm đó vẫn còn liên quan đến thời điểm nói. So sánh: 'I saw the trailer yesterday' (quá khứ đơn, có thời gian cụ thể) và 'I've seen the trailer' (hiện tại hoàn thành, không cần biết khi nào).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The present perfect (have/has + past participle) is used for experiences or information when the exact time is not important or not known. 'I've heard it's really funny' — at some point in the past, information was received. 'I've seen the trailer' — the experience happened before now. Compare: 'I saw the trailer yesterday' (simple past — specific time) vs 'I've seen the trailer' (present perfect — the experience, no specific time). This difference is very important in spoken English.

grammarNotes.title: Expressing preferences: 'I really want to', 'I was thinking more of', 'I'm okay with'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong hội thoại, chúng ta dùng nhiều cấu trúc khác nhau để diễn đạt sở thích và mức độ đồng ý. 'I really want to watch an action movie.' — Tôi rất muốn xem phim hành động. 'I was thinking more of a comedy.' — Tôi đang nghĩ đến phim hài hơn. 'I'm okay with comedy.' — Tôi ổn với phim hài (chấp nhận được). 'That works for me.' — Được với tôi. 'I'm not really in the mood for action.' — Tôi không thực sự có tâm trạng xem phim hành động. Các cách diễn đạt này thể hiện sự linh hoạt và lịch sự khi thương lượng.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: This dialogue shows a spectrum of preference expressions: strong preference — 'I really want to'; mild preference — 'I was thinking more of'; neutral acceptance — 'I'm okay with', 'That works for me'; mild reluctance — 'I'm not really in the mood for'; strong dislike — 'I didn't enjoy it at all.' Recognising these degrees helps you understand how strongly someone feels about something. 'I'm okay with X' does not mean enthusiastic — it means X is acceptable.

translationVi:
Tom: Này Sara, bạn có muốn xem phim tối nay không? Tôi không có kế hoạch gì cả.
Sara: Được chứ, nghe hay đó! Tôi vừa nghĩ điều tương tự. Bạn muốn xem gì?
Tom: Tôi thực sự muốn xem phim hành động. Có một phim mới vừa ra — nó có đánh giá rất tốt.
Sara: Hmm, tôi không thực sự có tâm trạng xem hành động tối nay. Tôi đang nghĩ đến phim hài hoặc phim tình cảm.
Tom: Phim tình cảm? Thôi nào, chúng ta lúc nào cũng xem phim tình cảm. Chúng ta không thể thử cái gì khác sao?
Sara: Không đúng! Lần trước chúng ta xem phim tội phạm hồi hộp mà bạn chọn, và tôi không thích chút nào.
Tom: Được rồi, hợp lý thôi. Vậy phim hài thì sao? Tôi ổn với phim hài.
Sara: Tôi cũng được. Bạn có nghĩ đến phim cụ thể nào không?
Tom: Có phim mới kia về hai người bạn đi road trip. Tôi nghe nói nó rất buồn cười.
Sara: Ồ, tôi đã xem trailer của phim đó rồi! Trông có vẻ hài hước lắm. Phim dài bao lâu?
Tom: Tôi nghĩ khoảng một tiếng năm mươi phút. Vậy chúng ta sẽ xem xong khoảng mười giờ, ổn thôi.
Sara: Hoàn hảo. Chúng ta có thể làm bỏng ngô không? Xem phim mà không có bỏng ngô thì không giống nhau.
Tom: Tất nhiên rồi. Tôi sẽ làm trong khi bạn tìm phim. Đồng ý không?
Sara: Đồng ý! Tôi cũng sẽ lấy đồ uống từ tủ lạnh. Hẹn gặp bạn trong phòng khách trong năm phút.

questions.prompt: What does Tom want to watch?
questions.explanation: 'I really want to watch an action movie.'
questions.hint: Tom states his preference clearly at the start.

questions.prompt: What kind of movie is Sara in the mood for?
questions.explanation: 'I was thinking more of a comedy or maybe a romance.'

questions.prompt: Why does Tom object to watching a romance?
options: He says they always watch romances
questions.explanation: 'we always watch romances. Can't we try something different?'
questions.hint: His objection is about frequency, not quality.

questions.prompt: What genre did they watch last time?
questions.explanation: 'Last time we watched that crime thriller you chose.'
questions.hint: Sara uses 'last time' to give an example.

questions.prompt: Did Sara enjoy the movie they watched last time?
options: Yes, but she found it a bit slow
No, she didn't enjoy it at all
questions.hint: She uses a strong negative expression.

questions.prompt: What genre do they finally agree on?
questions.explanation: 'What about a comedy then? I'm okay with comedy.' — Sara: 'That works for me.'
questions.hint: Tom makes a compromise offer and Sara accepts.

questions.prompt: What is the movie they choose about?
options: Two friends who start a business
Two friends who go on a road trip
A family moving to a new city
questions.explanation: 'There's that new one with the two friends who go on a road trip.'

questions.prompt: Has Sara seen the trailer for the movie?
questions.explanation: 'I've seen the trailer for that!' — using the present perfect shows she has had this experience.
questions.hint: She recognises the movie from the description.

questions.prompt: How did the trailer look to Sara?

questions.explanation: 'I think it's about one hour fifty minutes.'

questions.prompt: What does Tom offer to do?
options: Find the movie on the TV
questions.explanation: 'I'll make it while you find the movie.' — Tom will make the popcorn.
questions.hint: They divide the tasks between them.

questions.prompt: What will Sara get from the fridge?
questions.explanation: 'I'll also grab some drinks from the fridge.'
questions.hint: She adds a task for herself.

cloze.template:
Tom: Hey Sara, do you want to watch a movie tonight? I don't have any {{b1}}.
Sara: Sure! What do you feel like watching?
Tom: I really want to watch an {{b2}} movie. There's a new one with great reviews.
Sara: I'm not really in the {{b3}} for action tonight. I was thinking more of a comedy or maybe a romance.
Tom: A romance? We always watch romances. Can't we try something {{b4}}?
Sara: Last time we watched that crime {{b5}} you chose, and I didn't enjoy it at all.
Tom: Okay, {{b6}} enough. What about a comedy then? I'm okay with comedy.
Sara: That works for me. Do you have anything {{b7}} in mind?
Tom: There's that new one with two friends who go on a {{b8}}. I've heard it's really funny.
Sara: Oh, I've seen the {{b9}} for that! It looked hilarious. How long is it?
Tom: About one hour fifty minutes. We'd finish around {{b10}}, which is fine.
Sara: Perfect. Can we make some {{b11}}? A movie isn't the same without it.
Tom: Absolutely. I'll make it while you find the movie. {{b12}}?
Sara: Deal! I'll also grab some drinks from the fridge.

blanks.explanation: 'plans' — 'I don't have any plans' means he has no other commitments.
'action' — Tom specifically wants to watch an action movie.
'mood' — 'in the mood for' means feeling like doing something.
'different' — Tom wants to watch something different from their usual choice.
'thriller' — a crime thriller is a tense, suspenseful crime story.
'fair' — 'fair enough' is an expression meaning 'that's reasonable, I accept that'.
'specific' — 'anything specific in mind' means a particular film she is thinking of.
'road trip' — a road trip is a long journey by car, usually as a leisure activity.
'trailer' — a trailer is a short promotional video for a film.
'ten' — Tom says they would finish around ten o'clock.
'popcorn' — Sara says 'a movie isn't the same without popcorn'.
'Deal?' — asking 'Deal?' is an informal way of saying 'Do we have an agreement?'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Tom and Sara have different movie preferences but manage to reach a compromise. In relationships and friendships, we often have to find a middle ground. Do you think it is important to compromise on small things like choosing entertainment? How do you handle situations where you and a friend or family member disagree on what to do?
